finding: `identity set --actor "Codex-GPT5.6 Terra"` writes a per-actor profile, but `identity show` still resolves the legacy environment value `codex-gpt-5.4-mini` first and reports no repo default identity. This leaves a valid active task session attributed to Terra while generic commands may select a different actor.
expectedBehavior: Explicit identity must be durable and take precedence over stale legacy editor environment data, or commands must fail closed with an unambiguous mismatch diagnostic.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed in TASK-AAO-0173: repo-default identity now outranks legacy `AGENT_IDENTITY`, while explicit actor and `ATM_ACTOR_ID` remain higher precedence. `node --strip-types tests/cli/identity-per-actor-routing.test.ts` and `npm run typecheck` passed.
followUp: Keep the precedence regression and actor-resolution diagnostic stable for Captain/Team Broker UX.
